front.title: MPEG Media Transport (MMT) Packaging for Media over QUIC

abstract.t: This document specifies the use of MPEG Media Transport (MMT) as a container format for Media over QUIC (MoQ). It defines the mapping of MMTP packets to MoQ objects, Application-Layer FEC integration with multi-path repair passthrough across ATSC 3.0, SSM multicast, and MoQ CDN paths, bidirectional conversion between S-TSID and MoQ catalogs, and compatibility with ATSC 3.0 and ARIB STD-B60 systems.

section.name: Introduction
section.t: MPEG Media Transport (MMT) delivers multimedia over heterogeneous networks. It is supported in ATSC 3.0 (Americas, South Korea) alongside ROUTE/DASH, and is the basis for ARIB STD-B60 (Japan). MMT provides Media Processing Units (MPU) as the media container, Application-Layer FEC (AL-FEC), cross-layer signaling, and hybrid broadcast/broadband delivery.
This document defines how MMT-encapsulated media is transported over MoQ , enabling:

ol.li: Unified FEC via MoQ repair tracks per

section.name: Relationship to Other MoQ Media Formats
section.t: MMT packaging is RECOMMENDED when ingesting ATSC 3.0 or ARIB STD-B60 broadcasts, when FEC protection is needed for multicast, or when interoperability with broadcast receivers is required.
CMAF packaging (CMSF/CARP) is RECOMMENDED when content originates as DASH/HLS without multicast delivery requirements.

section.name: Terminology
section.t: The key words "MUST", "MUST NOT", "REQUIRED", "SHALL", "SHALL NOT", "SHOULD", "SHOULD NOT", "RECOMMENDED", "NOT RECOMMENDED", "MAY", and "OPTIONAL" in this document are to be interpreted as described in BCP 14 when, and only when, they appear in all capitals, as shown here.

dl.dd: MMT Protocol -- the packet layer of MMT (ISO 23008-1 Clause 8)
Media Processing Unit -- a self-contained media segment, typically aligned with a GOP
Media Fragment Unit -- a single coded media frame within an MPU
Service-based Transport Session Instance Description -- ATSC 3.0 signaling table with transport and FEC parameters
Service Layer Table -- ATSC 3.0 bootstrap signaling
MMT Package Table -- signaling table with MMT asset info
MMT Presentation Information -- presentation timing table

section.name: MMTP Header Format
section.artwork: MMTP Header (12 bytes minimum) { Version (2), Packet Counter Flag (C) (1), FEC Type (F) (2), Reserved (1), Extension Flag (X) (1), RAP Flag (R) (1), Packet Type (6), Packet ID (16), Timestamp (32), Packet Sequence Number (32), [Packet Counter (32)], // Present if C=1 [Header Extension (..)] // Present if X=1 }
section.t: Key fields for MoQ mapping:

dl.dd: Maps to MoQ track within namespace
UTC wallclock in NTP short format; maps to MoQ object timestamp
Maps to MoQ object ID within group

section.name: Object Payload
section.t: Each MoQ object carries one MMTP packet:

Publishers MAY strip the MMTP header if subscribers negotiate raw ISOBMFF delivery via the catalog "container" field. Stripping loses FEC Type, RAP flag, and timestamp metadata; receivers MUST then rely on catalog and MoQ object headers for this information.

For MoQ unicast delivery without ATSC 3.0/ARIB interoperability requirements, LOC with MoQ extension headers is RECOMMENDED over full MMTP encapsulation. LOC eliminates ~30 bytes of per-object MMTP overhead that is redundant over MoQ (packet_id, timestamp, sequence_number, MPU fields, MFU DU header are all provided by MoQ transport framing and LOC extensions). MMTP encapsulation (container value 'mmtp') is RECOMMENDED when multicast/broadcast interoperability is required, as each UDP packet needs self-describing per-packet metadata.

section.name: Group Boundaries
section.t: Group boundaries align with MPU boundaries:

ul.li: Group N contains all MFUs of MPU N
Object 0 of each group contains the MPU metadata (mmpu/moov boxes)
The first object of each group SHOULD have RAP Flag = 1

section.name: Unified Ordering Model
section.t: The MPU Sequence Number is the single authoritative ordering key across media decode, FEC recovery, and MoQ transport:
section.artwork: MPU Sequence Number (mpuSeq) | +-- MoQ Group ID (mpuSeq -> Group ID) | \-- Decode order -- H.264/H.265 requires frames in sequence | +-- FEC Source Block (SBN = mpuSeq / K, per I-D.ramadan-moq-fec) | \-- ESI alignment -- source symbols map to sequential mpuSeqs | \-- MMTP Timestamp (wallclock presentation time) \-- Presentation -- monotonic within decode order
section.t: Receivers MUST track the last decoded mpuSeq and:

ul.li: Keyframes (RAP=1): Always decode; reset decode-order tracking
Delta frames (RAP=0): Decode only if mpuSeq > lastDecodedMpuSeq; drop out-of-order delta frames to prevent reference frame mismatch
FEC repair: Use mpuSeq to derive SBN for block alignment

section.t: This ordering model applies identically across MoQ unicast (QUIC streams), SSM multicast (UDP), and ATSC 3.0 broadcast (ROUTE/MMTP). The decoder MUST enforce mpuSeq ordering regardless of delivery path.
Receivers SHOULD NOT rely on transport-layer ordering. Multicast and broadcast paths may reorder MFUs due to FEC interleaving, network path reordering, or relay buffering.

Fragment reassembly (FI=1,2,3 within a single MFU) and decode ordering (mpuSeq between MFUs) are separate concerns. The reassembler buffers fragments by fragment counter; the decoder tracks lastDecodedMpuSeq and drops out-of-order delta frames.

section.name: MFU Mode
section.t: For ultra-low-latency delivery, MMT supports MFU mode where each video frame is a separate MoQ object:
section.artwork:
Standard MPU Mode: Object 0: [MMTP][MPU: mmpu+moov+moof+mdat containing all frames]
MFU Mode: Object 0: [MMTP][MPU metadata: mmpu+moov+moof header] Object 1: [MMTP][MFU: IDR frame NALUs] Object 2: [MMTP][MFU: P frame NALUs] Object 3: [MMTP][MFU: P frame NALUs] ...
section.t: MFU mode enables per-frame FEC protection, frame-level prioritization (IDR vs P/B), and lower end-to-end latency.

section.name: MFU Fragmentation
section.t: When MFU size exceeds typical MTU (1200-1400 bytes for QUIC), publishers SHOULD:

ol.li: Fragment MFU across multiple MMTP packets
Set Packet Counter Flag (C=1) for reassembly tracking
Publish all fragments as a single MoQ object
Include the complete MFU in the object payload

section.t: MMTP fragmentation is transparent to MoQ; each MoQ object represents a complete, potentially multi-packet MFU. Receivers reassemble using Packet Counter and Packet Sequence Number before media processing.

section.name: FEC Integration
section.t: MMT AL-FEC supports RaptorQ , LDPC , and Reed-Solomon , with parameters signaled via MMTP signaling messages or S-TSID (ATSC 3.0). For MoQ, FEC repair uses the model in :
section.artwork:
Source Track: video \-- Objects: MMTP packets with FEC Type=0 or 1 (source)
Repair Track: video/repair \-- Objects: MMTP packets with FEC Type=2 (repair, passthrough)
section.t: Repair track objects carry raw MMTP repair packets without modification, using Repair Container 0x01 ("mmtp") per Section 4.6. MoQ relays pass MMTP repair packets through as-is.

This passthrough design enables multi-path FEC: the same MMTP repair packets are delivered identically via ATSC 3.0 RF broadcast, SSM multicast, and MoQ CDN. Receivers implement one FEC repair parser for all paths. See Appendix C for the byte-offset extraction recipe.

section.name: Interleaving
section.t: MMT AL-FEC interleaves source symbols across multiple MFUs:
Typical interleave depths:

section.name: OTI Signaling
section.t: The S-TSID table contains RaptorQ OTI (Object Transmission Info):
For MoQ, OTI is signaled via FEC_CONFIG per Section 4.

section.name: FEC_CONFIG Usage
section.t: The FEC_CONFIG message format is defined normatively in Section 4.1. This section specifies MMT-specific considerations.

section.name: MMT-Specific Fields
section.t: When used with MMT packaging, FEC_CONFIG fields map as follows:

dl.dd: Number of MFUs (or MMTP packets) per FEC block
Number of MPU frames per FEC block, matching the original broadcast FEC interleave depth
For RaptorQ, the 12-byte concatenation of Common FEC OTI and Scheme-Specific FEC OTI per

section.t: When Repair Container is 0x01 (MMTP), per-packet OTI embedded in each MMTP repair packet (12 bytes at byte offset 21) provides immediate decoder configuration. FEC_CONFIG is therefore OPTIONAL for MMTP container mode; if sent, its fields SHOULD match the per-packet OTI values.

section.name: Repair Track Discovery
section.t: When FEC is enabled, repair tracks use the naming convention in Section 6.1:
section.artwork: Source Track: [namespace, track_name] Repair Track: [namespace, track_name, "repair"]
section.t: The subscriber MUST subscribe to the repair track separately. The repair track uses lower priority (typically 7) so repair symbols are dropped first under congestion.

section.name: Multicast FEC Signaling
section.t: For multicast delivery without bidirectional signaling, FEC_CONFIG parameters are conveyed via:

ol.li: MMTP AL-FEC Signaling (message_id=0x0203): In-band delivery per ISO/IEC 23008-1:2023 Amendment 1:2025
MoQ Catalog Extension: Out-of-band via catalog JSON:

section.name: Multicast Integration
section.t: MMT content can be delivered via IP multicast (SSM, AMT) and TreeDN. Platform-specific delivery paths, SSM group allocation, TreeDN integration, and home gateway architecture are defined in .
Over multicast, MMTP packets are transmitted as UDP datagrams with the standard MMTP header intact. MoQ relays at network edges bridge MMTP content into MoQ via QUIC/WebTransport.
For ATSC 3.0 and ARIB STD-B60 receivers, MMTP over SSM is the native delivery path and requires no protocol translation.

section.name: ARIB STD-B60 Compatibility
section.t: ARIB STD-B60 (Japan) uses the same ISO 23008-1 foundation as ATSC 3.0 with the following considerations.

section.name: Clock Reference
section.t: ARIB STD-B60 uses UTC wallclock timestamps in NTP short format (32-bit: 16-bit seconds + 16-bit fractional seconds):
section.artwork: MoQ Timestamp (seconds) = MMTP Timestamp upper 16 bits + (lower 16 bits / 65536)
section.t: This differs from MPEG-2 TS, which uses a 90kHz PTS/DTS clock.

section.name: 8K UHDTV Support
section.t: ARIB STD-B60 supports 8K UHDTV (7680x4320) via HEVC Main 10 profile at Level 6.1. For efficient 8K delivery:

ol.li: MoQ Group boundaries SHOULD align with HEVC CTU rows for spatial random access
Multiple MoQ tracks MAY carry tile regions for parallel decode

section.t: Example 8K track structure:
section.artwork: namespace: "live/8k" tracks: - video/tile_0_0 (top-left) - video/tile_0_1 (top-right) - video/tile_1_0 (bottom-left) - video/tile_1_1 (bottom-right) - video/repair (FEC for all tiles)

section.name: Hybridcast Integration
section.t: ARIB Hybridcast enables companion device synchronization. When bridging Hybridcast services, publishers SHOULD:

ol.li: Include timeline alignment metadata in MoQ catalog
Signal synchronization points via MoQ object timestamps

section.t: Catalog extension for Hybridcast:

section.name: Typical FEC Parameters
section.t: Publishers SHOULD preserve original FEC parameters when ingesting ARIB STD-B60 content.

section.name: Transport Hierarchy
section.t: Clients SHOULD attempt transports in preference order. The transport hierarchy is defined in Section 6.
For MMT deployments, AL-FEC () is essential on SSM/AMT paths since there is no retransmission. On MoQ/QUIC paths, FEC reduces retransmission latency but QUIC provides reliable fallback.

section.name: Catalog Signaling
section.t: The MoQ catalog indicates MMT container format and multicast endpoints.

section.name: Container Values
section.t: Example:
When FEC is enabled for 'isobmff' or 'mfu' containers over MoQ unicast, source track objects SHOULD carry the FEC Payload ID and Object Length extension headers defined in .

section.name: S-TSID to MoQ Catalog Conversion
section.t: When ingesting ATSC 3.0 content, generate MoQ catalog from the S-TSID signaling table (). For MMT-delivered content, equivalent parameters come from MMTP signaling messages (MPT/MPI):
section.artwork: S-TSID Input: <S-TSID> <RS sIpAddr="192.168.1.100" dIpAddr="232.1.1.50" dPort="5000"> <LS tsi="1" bw="5000000"> <SrcFlow rt="true"> <ContentInfo> <MediaInfo contentType="video" repId="1080p"/> </ContentInfo> <Payload codePoint="128" formatId="2"/> </SrcFlow> <RepairFlow> <FECParameters maximumDelay="1000" overhead="25" fecOTI="K=32;T=1312;Z=4"> <ProtectedObject tsi="1"> <SourceTOI x="0" y="255"/> </ProtectedObject> </FECParameters> </RepairFlow> </LS> </RS> </S-TSID>
section.t: The "multicast" field uses the extended format in Section 7.2. Conversion rules:

section.name: MoQ Catalog to S-TSID Conversion
section.t: When generating ATSC-compatible output, convert MoQ catalog to S-TSID:
section.artwork: S-TSID Output: <S-TSID xmlns="tag:atsc.org,2016:XMLSchemas/ATSC3/Delivery/S-TSID/1.0/"> <RS sIpAddr="192.168.1.100" dIpAddr="232.1.1.50" dPort="5000"> <LS tsi="1" bw="5000000"> <SrcFlow rt="true" minBuffSize="5000000"> <ContentInfo> <MediaInfo contentType="video"/> </ContentInfo> <Payload codePoint="128" formatId="2" srcFecPayloadId="6"/> </SrcFlow> <RepairFlow> <FECParameters maximumDelay="133" overhead="25" fecOTI="F=32;T=1312;Z=4;N=1;Al=8"> </FECParameters> </RepairFlow> </LS> </RS> </S-TSID>
section.t: Conversion rules:

section.name: Multicast Endpoint Catalog Extension
section.t: The multicast catalog extension is defined in Section 7.
When converting S-TSID to MoQ catalog (), the "multicast" field MUST conform to the extended format in Section 7.2.

section.name: Security Considerations
section.t: MMT content protection uses Common Encryption (CENC), which is preserved through MoQ transport. The MMTP header is not encrypted, allowing relays to inspect packet type and sequence without accessing media content.
Multicast-specific security (source authentication, replay protection, AMT relay trust) is defined in Section 8.

section.name: IANA Considerations
section.t: This document requests registration of container format identifiers in the "MoQ Container Formats" registry:
This document also requests registration of MoQ message type (shared with ):

front.title: Key words for use in RFCs to Indicate Requirement Levels
Ambiguity of Uppercase vs Lowercase in RFC 2119 Key Words
Information technology - High efficiency coding and media delivery in heterogeneous environments - Part 1: MPEG media transport (MMT)
RaptorQ Forward Error Correction Scheme for Object Delivery
Forward Error Correction for Media over QUIC
Low Density Parity Check (LDPC) Staircase and Triangle Forward Error Correction (FEC) Schemes
Signaling, Delivery, Synchronization, and Error Protection
MMT-Based Media Transport Scheme in Digital Broadcasting Systems
Multicast Delivery and Endpoint Discovery for Media over QUIC

section.name: Per-Object Overhead (Recurring)
section.t: LOC source objects need zero additional FEC extensions -- SBN and ESI are derived from MoQ group_id and object_id.

section.name: Total Overhead Analysis
section.t: For a 30fps stream with K=32 source symbols per FEC block:

section.name: S-TSID Conversion Example
section.t: Complete bidirectional conversion between ATSC S-TSID and MoQ catalog for a multi-track service.

section.name: Original ATSC S-TSID
section.artwork: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <S-TSID xmlns="tag:atsc.org,2016:XMLSchemas/ATSC3/Delivery/S-TSID/1.0/"> <RS sIpAddr="10.0.0.1" dIpAddr="232.1.1.10" dPort="5000"> <LS tsi="1" bw="8000000"> <SrcFlow rt="true" minBuffSize="8000000"> <ContentInfo> <MediaInfo contentType="video" repId="1080p" lang="en"/> </ContentInfo> <EFDT> <FDT-Instance Expires="4294967295"> <File Content-Location="video/1080p/init.mp4" TOI="1"/> </FDT-Instance> </EFDT> <Payload codePoint="128" formatId="2" srcFecPayloadId="6"/> </SrcFlow> <RepairFlow> <FECParameters maximumDelay="1000" overhead="25" fecOTI="F=32;T=1312;Z=30;N=1;Al=8"> <ProtectedObject tsi="1"> <SourceTOI x="0" y="65535"/> </ProtectedObject> </FECParameters> </RepairFlow> </LS> <LS tsi="2" bw="128000"> <SrcFlow rt="true"> <ContentInfo> <MediaInfo contentType="audio" repId="stereo" lang="en"/> </ContentInfo> <Payload codePoint="128" formatId="2"/> </SrcFlow> </LS> </RS> </S-TSID>
